headline: Micron Selloff Was a ‘Buying Opportunity’—But the Stock Continues to Fall
summary: Micron Technology  stock fell sharply Tuesday as investors resumed selling shares of memory-chip makers, which took a beating in last week’s brutal artificial-intelligence selloff.  Shares of Micron fell 4.4% to $907.22, having surged 9.9% the previous session.  The losses Tuesday mean Micron is trading about 15% below where it closed last Wednesday, before so-so revenue guidance from chip maker  Broadcom  led to investors slamming the brakes on the AI trade.

headline: More swings for AI stocks drag Wall Street back on the roller coaster
summary: Another  sudden reversal for high-flying artificial-intelligence  stocks sent Wall Street reeling on Tuesday.  The S&P 500 fell 0.3% after careening between an initial gain of 1% and a midday loss of 2.3%, pulling further from its all-time high  set a week ago.  After similar yo-yo moves, the Dow Jones Industrial Average added 86 points, or 0.2%, and the Nasdaq composite dropped 1%.

content.title: More swings for AI stocks drag Wall Street back on the roller coaster
content.summary: Another  sudden reversal for high-flying artificial-intelligence  stocks sent Wall Street reeling on Tuesday.  The S&P 500 fell 0.3% after careening between an initial gain of 1% and a midday loss of 2.3%, pulling further from its all-time high  set a week ago.  After similar yo-yo moves, the Dow Jones Industrial Average added 86 points, or 0.2%, and the Nasdaq composite dropped 1%.

thumbnail.caption: Options trader Chris Daytona, right, works on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange, Wednesday, June 3, 2026. (AP Photo/Richard Drew)
